expectations.text: Calls get_session_context() MCP tool to check for existing session state
expectations.evidence: Lines 17-23: Step 1 explicitly calls mcp__ohno__get_session_context() with stated purpose 'Check for any previous session state, in-progress tasks, or blockers.'

expectations.text: Calls get_next_task() to find the first available task
expectations.evidence: Lines 65-69: Step 5 calls mcp__ohno__get_next_task() to get the first task after session initialization.

expectations.text: References semi-auto mode behavior (pause at story boundaries, skip task boundaries)
expectations.evidence: Lines 7, 110-119: Describes semi-auto as 'pausing at story and epic boundaries so you can review, but continuing automatically between individual tasks.' Task boundaries get 'Task [title] complete. Moving to next task...' while story boundaries get a pause with summary and prompt.

expectations.text: Mentions worktree setup or isolation for the task
expectations.evidence: Lines 149: Response explicitly states 'No worktree management. Claude would not create isolated git worktrees for feature/bug tasks.' Worktrees are only mentioned as a gap that would be missing without the skill, not as something the baseline would actually do.

expectations.text: Dispatches an implementer subagent for the first task
expectations.evidence: Lines 88-89: Response explicitly states 'Without skill guidance, Claude would not dispatch a subagent. It would implement the task directly in the conversation context.' Also line 137: 'No subagent dispatch' listed as Critical Gap #1.

expectations.text: Describes the checkpoint behavior for story completion
expectations.evidence: Lines 114-119: Describes story completion checkpoint as pausing with summary ('Story [title] complete (X/X tasks done). Here's a summary of what was built') and asking user whether to continue, review, or stop.
